title: Rheumatoid Arthritis (RA)
root_mechanism: Molecular mimicry or gut dysbiosis → loss of immune tolerance → Th1/Th17 cell activation → synovial macrophage infiltration → TNF-α, IL-1β, IL-6 excess → synovitis, pannus formation, cartilage erosion, and systemic inflammation.

interventions.intervention: Warm paraffin bath or warm water soak for hands/feet
interventions.physiological_effect: Heat reduces synovial viscosity; promotes synovial fluid circulation; reduces morning stiffness by dilating periarticular capillaries

interventions.intervention: Gentle joint mobilisation — range-of-motion exercises all joints
interventions.physiological_effect: Passive-to-active ROM preserves cartilage nutrition (avascular cartilage feeds via synovial fluid diffusion during movement)
Reduces systemic cortisol → lowers IL-6 and CRP; vagal activation reduces pro-inflammatory macrophage polarisation

interventions.intervention: Anti-inflammatory: fatty fish/flaxseed + turmeric milk + berries
interventions.physiological_effect: Omega-3 EPA/DHA competitively inhibit arachidonic acid → reduce leukotriene B4 and PGE2; turmeric curcumin blocks NF-kB

interventions.intervention: Cold pack on acutely inflamed joints; hot pack for stiff joints — clinician choice
interventions.physiological_effect: Cold on inflamed: reduces prostaglandin synthesis; slows nerve conduction → analgesia. Hot on stiff: reduces adhesions, increases extensibility
Boswellic acid inhibits 5-lipoxygenase → blocks leukotriene B4 (primary neutrophil chemoattractant in RA); ginger gingerols inhibit COX-2

interventions.intervention: Anti-inflammatory Mediterranean: olive oil, vegetables, whole grains, legumes, no red meat
interventions.physiological_effect: Mediterranean diet reduces CRP 20%; olive oil oleocanthal = natural ibuprofen equivalent; avoids arachidonic acid from red meat

interventions.intervention: Gentle 20-min pool walk or chair yoga
interventions.physiological_effect: Aquatic exercise offloads joints (90% body weight supported); maintains muscle strength without synovial trauma

interventions.intervention: Castor oil joint pack on affected joints + medicated Mahanarayan oil massage
interventions.physiological_effect: Castor oil ricinoleic acid reduces PGE2; Mahanarayan oil sesame + 50 herb blend penetrates periarticular tissue
Gentle yoga reduces TNF-α by 18% (documented); improves joint proprioception; HPA axis regulation
Magnesium sulfate reduces synovial prostaglandins; sulfate supports cartilage GAG synthesis

interventions.intervention: Light anti-inflammatory dinner: dal khichdi + turmeric + ghee
interventions.physiological_effect: Ghee butyrate reduces intestinal permeability (leaky gut drives RA autoimmunity); turmeric synergises with fat for absorption

interventions.intervention: Medicated herbal oil massage (Dhanwantharam oil) on all joints
interventions.physiological_effect: Sesame oil base transports anti-inflammatory alkaloids across skin-joint barrier

practices.maintenance_dose: Lifelong — no red meat, no refined sugar

relapse_rules.action: Return to Phase 2 + daily castor packs

dietary_protocol.eat_window: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM (regular meals; omega-3 with every main meal)
dietary_protocol.meal_order: Omega-3 rich foods first → Protein → Anti-inflammatory vegetables → Complex carbs last

fasting_rules.physiological_effect: Overnight fast reduces inflammatory cytokine production; reduces fasting IL-6 and TNF-α which peak in early morning causing RA morning stiffness
Boswellic acid pre-meal timing ensures 5-LOX inhibition during peak post-meal inflammatory response
Consistent omega-3 competitively inhibits arachidonic acid at COX and LOX enzymes reducing synovial eicosanoids

fasting_rules.specification: Eliminate tomato, eggplant, bell pepper for 6 weeks; reintroduce one at a time
fasting_rules.physiological_effect: Solanine alkaloids may trigger gut permeability → increased antigen load → autoimmune flare in susceptible RA patients
Butyrate seals intestinal tight junctions → reduces leaky gut → reduces antigen translocation driving RA autoimmunity

phase_macros.carbohydrates: Moderate: 40–45% (anti-inflammatory whole grains; no refined carbs; no sugar)
phase_macros.protein: Moderate: 20–25% (fatty fish 3×/week; plant proteins; collagen-rich bone broth)
phase_macros.fat: Moderate-high: 30–35% (omega-3 dominant: fish oil, flaxseed, walnuts; olive oil; ghee)
phase_macros.rationale: Anti-inflammatory Mediterranean macro pattern; maximise omega-3:omega-6 ratio; eliminate all arachidonic acid sources

phase_macros.carbohydrates: Moderate: 45% (increase fermented foods; more legumes for gut microbiome repair)
phase_macros.protein: Moderate: 20–25% (add collagen peptides for cartilage support)
phase_macros.rationale: Gut microbiome repair phase; increasing probiotic and prebiotic foods; collagen supplementation supports cartilage matrix repair
Lifelong anti-inflammatory Mediterranean diet; omega-3 supervised; no red meat or refined sugar

items: Turmeric (1 tsp with black pepper daily)
Red meat — beef, mutton, pork (arachidonic acid)
Refined omega-6 oils — sunflower, corn in excess

meals.what_to_eat: Warm water + turmeric (1/2 tsp) + black pepper + ginger decoction (1 cup)
meals.physiological_rationale: Triple COX-2 and 5-LOX inhibition before joints load-bear; warm water maintains joint warmth reducing morning stiffness

meals.what_to_eat: Warm water soak for hands/feet (15 min) then gentle ROM exercises all joints
meals.physiological_rationale: Paraffin/warm water reduces synovial viscosity; ROM distributes synovial fluid to cartilage before breakfast loading

meals.what_to_eat: Oats porridge (no sugar) + 2 tbsp ground flaxseed + walnuts (4) + berries + turmeric milk (no sugar)
meals.physiological_rationale: Flaxseed ALA omega-3; walnuts DHA/EPA; berries anthocyanins block TNF-α; oats beta-glucan reduces systemic inflammation

meals.what_to_eat: Boswellia + ginger decoction (1 cup) — 20 min before eating
meals.physiological_rationale: 5-LOX inhibition via boswellic acid peaks at joint tissue 20–30 min post-ingestion

meals.what_to_eat: Grilled salmon (150 g) + olive oil salad (leafy greens, broccoli, cherry) + small ragi roti + 1 tsp ghee + probiotic curd
meals.physiological_rationale: Salmon EPA/DHA most potent dietary anti-inflammatory; oleocanthal inhibits COX; ghee seals gut; curd repairs microbiome

meals.what_to_eat: Tart cherry juice (150 mL) + collagen peptides (10 g) or bone broth (1 cup)
meals.physiological_rationale: Tart cherry anthocyanins reduce CRP and joint pain; collagen provides hydroxyproline for cartilage repair

meals.what_to_eat: Dal khichdi (moong dal + rice) + turmeric + 1 tsp ghee + cooked spinach
meals.physiological_rationale: Ghee butyrate seals gut tight junctions overnight; turmeric bioavailability enhanced by fat; easy-to-digest dinner minimises overnight gut inflammation

meals.what_to_eat: Dhanwantharam/Mahanarayan oil massage on all joints (15 min)
meals.physiological_rationale: Overnight transdermal anti-inflammatory alkaloid penetration to periarticular tissue during rest

functional_foods.name: Boswellia (Shallaki)
functional_foods.dose_timing: 400 mg standardised extract — 20 min before meals, 2×/day; supervised
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: AKBA inhibits 5-lipoxygenase → blocks leukotriene B4 (primary RA neutrophil chemoattractant); reduces joint swelling and morning stiffness

functional_foods.name: Ginger (Zingiber officinale)
functional_foods.dose_timing: 1-inch fresh in decoction 3×/day or 1 g dried in cooking
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: 6-Gingerol inhibits COX-2 and 5-LOX; suppresses TNF-α; documented equivalent joint pain relief to ibuprofen (RCT 2015)

functional_foods.name: Turmeric + black pepper
functional_foods.dose_timing: 1 tsp curcumin + 1/4 tsp black pepper with fat — daily
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Curcumin blocks NF-kB → suppresses TNF-α, IL-1β, IL-6; reduces CRP and DAS28 in RA (multiple RCTs); piperine increases bioavailability 2000%

functional_foods.name: Mahanarayan oil
functional_foods.dose_timing: Warm massage on all joints — 15 min daily Phase 1; 3×/week Phase 2–3
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Sesame base with 50+ herbs transdermally; reduces periarticular inflammation; improves joint circulation

functional_foods.name: Tart cherry juice
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Anthocyanins inhibit COX-1 and COX-2; reduce CRP and IL-6 in inflammatory arthritis

functional_foods.name: Collagen peptides
functional_foods.dose_timing: 10 g hydrolysed collagen in warm water — daily (between meals)
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Provides hydroxyproline and glycine for cartilage matrix; improves synovial fluid viscosity; reduces joint pain

functional_foods.name: Castor oil (joint pack)
functional_foods.dose_timing: Warm cloth soaked in castor oil on joints — 20 min daily Phase 1
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Ricinoleic acid binds EP3 receptors → reduces synovial PGE2; increases local lymphatic drainage

functional_foods.name: Omega-3 (fish oil)
functional_foods.dose_timing: 3 g EPA+DHA from fatty fish 3×/week; supplement if insufficient
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: EPA/DHA shift eicosanoid production from PGE2/LTB4 to anti-inflammatory PGE3/LTB5; reduce DAS28, CRP, ESR, morning stiffness (Cochrane 2012)

asanas.physiological_effect: Maintains shoulder ROM; synovial fluid distribution to glenohumeral joints; prevents adhesive capsulitis
Systematic joint mobilisation from toes to neck; distributes synovial fluid to avascular cartilage; classic RA therapy
Reduces lower limb oedema via gravity-assisted drainage; anti-gravity joint decompression
Reduces TNF-α via cortisol regulation; pain modulation via default mode network downregulation
Spinal facet mobilisation; reduces systemic inflammatory cytokines
Most potent anti-inflammatory pranayama — reduces IL-6 directly

red_flags.action: Rule out septic arthritis — joint aspiration required urgently
Rule out RA-associated interstitial lung disease
Rule out scleritis or uveitis — urgent ophthalmology
Rule out atlanto-axial subluxation in cervical RA

clinical_disclaimer: This protocol is designed for use under qualified BNYS clinical supervision in collaboration with a rheumatologist. Do not modify DMARDs or biologics without physician oversight. Boswellia and omega-3 require clinician approval with anticoagulants. This document is for clinical reference only.
